treatments.name: DA-EPOCH-R (Dose-Adjusted Etoposide, Prednisone, Vincristine, Cyclophosphamide, Doxorubicin + Rituximab)

evidence_level.rationale: NCI 9177 multicenter Phase 2 trial with 113 patients; HOVON/SAKK Phase 3 trial confirming non-inferiority to R-CODOX-M/IVAC; published in NEJM

survival_benefit.rationale: 4-year EFS 84.5%, 4-year OS 87.0%; low-risk patients achieve 100% 4-year EFS; cures most adult BL patients irrespective of HIV status

accessibility.rationale: All components FDA approved; available at hematology centers worldwide; dose-adjusted protocol allows safer outpatient administration in some cycles

safety_profile.rationale: Significantly fewer infectious complications, transfusions, and hospitalization days compared to R-CODOX-M/IVAC; dose adjustment mechanism improves safety; still requires intensive monitoring

biomarker_match.rationale: Rituximab targets CD20 universally expressed in Burkitt lymphoma; MYC-driven proliferation addressed by continuous infusion schedule; effective in MYC-rearranged tumors

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Continuous infusion chemotherapy with dose adjustment based on neutrophil nadir targets MYC-driven rapidly proliferating cells throughout cell cycle. Etoposide inhibits topoisomerase II; doxorubicin intercalates DNA; cyclophosphamide alkylates DNA; vincristine disrupts microtubules; rituximab provides anti-CD20 immunotherapy. Dose adjustment optimizes between efficacy and toxicity.

key_evidence.journal: New England Journal of Medicine / Lancet Haematology

treatments.name: R-CODOX-M/R-IVAC (Rituximab + Magrath Protocol)

evidence_level.rationale: HOVON/SAKK Phase 3 trial; Magrath original protocol with 92% 2-year EFS; multiple international validation studies; most frequently used regimen worldwide

survival_benefit.rationale: 2-year PFS 76% in Phase 3 (high-risk); modified R-CODOX-M/IVAC shows 3-year EFS 81.2% and OS 92.1% in sporadic BL; CR rates 75-85%; ~65-80% cure rate

accessibility.rationale: All components widely available; requires experienced hematology center; intensive inpatient administration; higher resource utilization than DA-EPOCH-R

safety_profile.rationale: More toxic than DA-EPOCH-R: more infectious complications, transfusion requirements, and hospitalization days; high-dose methotrexate and cytarabine carry significant toxicity; mucositis and myelosuppression severe

biomarker_match.rationale: Rituximab targets CD20; intensive CNS-penetrating regimen addresses high CNS relapse risk in Burkitt lymphoma; MYC-rearranged tumors respond well to intensive multi-agent approach

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Alternating intensive chemotherapy cycles: CODOX-M (cyclophosphamide, vincristine, doxorubicin, high-dose methotrexate) alternates with IVAC (ifosfamide, etoposide, high-dose cytarabine). High-dose methotrexate and cytarabine provide CNS penetration. Rituximab adds anti-CD20 immunotherapy. Designed to prevent tumor regrowth between cycles.

treatments.name: R-Hyper-CVAD / R-MA (Rituximab + Hyper-CVAD alternating with Methotrexate/Cytarabine)

evidence_level.rationale: MD Anderson Phase 2 data with long-term follow-up; 79 adults with BL/HGBCL; well-established regimen but no Phase 3 comparison vs other intensive regimens

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 97%, CR 86-91%; 3-year OS 89%, DFS 88%, EFS 80%; 5-year OS 74% with rituximab vs 50% without (p=0.03); particularly improved outcomes in elderly (70% vs 19%)

accessibility.rationale: All components FDA approved; requires intensive inpatient care for 8 cycles; highly resource-intensive; predominantly used in North America (MD Anderson protocol)

safety_profile.rationale: Significant hematologic toxicity; secondary myelodysplastic syndrome/AML risk reported in 6 patients; 8 treatment cycles with prolonged treatment duration; intensive supportive care required

biomarker_match.rationale: Rituximab targets CD20; high-dose methotrexate and cytarabine provide CNS prophylaxis/treatment; effective in MYC-rearranged BL and high-risk features including BM/CNS involvement

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Alternating cycles of Hyper-CVAD (hyperfractionated cyclophosphamide, vincristine, doxorubicin, dexamethasone) with high-dose methotrexate and cytarabine, plus rituximab. Fractionated cyclophosphamide improves DNA damage; alternating high-dose antimetabolites provide intensive cell-cycle-specific killing and CNS penetration.

treatments.name: TLS Prophylaxis Protocol (Rasburicase + Aggressive Hydration)

evidence_level.rationale: NCCN/ASCO guidelines mandate TLS prophylaxis in Burkitt lymphoma; multiple clinical studies validate rasburicase; FDA-approved indication for hyperuricemia in hematologic malignancies

survival_benefit.rationale: TLS is life-threatening; Burkitt lymphoma is the highest-risk malignancy for TLS; prophylaxis reduces TLS-related mortality from 15-20% to <5%; prevents dialysis-requiring renal failure

accessibility.rationale: Rasburicase widely available; allopurinol universally accessible; IV hydration standard; rasburicase expensive but critical; G6PD screening required before rasburicase

safety_profile.rationale: Rasburicase: rare hemolysis in G6PD-deficient patients (screen required), anaphylaxis <1%; IV hydration carries minimal risk; allopurinol well-tolerated

biomarker_match.rationale: Burkitt lymphoma with Ki-67 ~100% and MYC rearrangement is the highest-risk category for TLS; mandatory prophylaxis regardless of initial labs due to 24-hour doubling time

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Rasburicase (recombinant urate oxidase) rapidly converts uric acid to allantoin, which is 5-10x more soluble; reduces uric acid within 4 hours. Combined with aggressive IV hydration (3L/m2/day) to maintain high urine output and prevent uric acid/calcium phosphate precipitation in renal tubules. Electrolyte monitoring and correction prevents fatal arrhythmias.

key_evidence.study_name: NCCN TLS Guidelines / COG Rasburicase Study
key_evidence.journal: Journal of Clinical Oncology / NCCN Guidelines

treatments.name: CNS Prophylaxis (Intrathecal Methotrexate + Cytarabine)

evidence_level.rationale: All major Burkitt lymphoma protocols incorporate CNS prophylaxis; without it 30-50% develop CNS relapse; NCCN mandates CNS prophylaxis for all Burkitt lymphoma patients

survival_benefit.rationale: Reduces CNS relapse from 30-50% to 6-11%; CNS involvement is the strongest negative prognostic factor (4-year EFS 45.5% with CNS vs 89.9% without); essential component of curative therapy

accessibility.rationale: Intrathecal administration requires lumbar puncture expertise; methotrexate and cytarabine widely available; high-dose systemic methotrexate provides additional CNS penetration

safety_profile.rationale: Intrathecal administration carries headache, arachnoiditis risk; high-dose systemic MTX requires leucovorin rescue and renal monitoring; cytarabine can cause cerebellar toxicity; procedural complications from lumbar punctures

biomarker_match.rationale: Mandatory for all Burkitt lymphoma regardless of CNS status; MYC-rearranged BL has particularly high CNS tropism; jaw presentation does not reduce CNS risk

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Intrathecal chemotherapy delivers methotrexate (antifolate) and cytarabine (nucleoside analog) directly into cerebrospinal fluid, bypassing the blood-brain barrier. IT therapy treats existing leptomeningeal disease and prevents CNS seeding. Combined with systemic high-dose methotrexate which achieves therapeutic CSF levels.

key_evidence.study_name: Multiple BL Protocol Studies / NCCN Guidelines

treatments.name: Axicabtagene Ciloleucel / Lisocabtagene Maraleucel (CAR-T Cell Therapy)

evidence_level.rationale: FDA approved for relapsed/refractory LBCL including high-grade B-cell lymphoma; limited specific Burkitt lymphoma data; case series and small studies support efficacy in R/R BL

survival_benefit.rationale: In R/R aggressive B-cell lymphoma: ORR 60-83%, CR 40-60%; 5-year OS ~40% for axi-cel in heavily pretreated LBCL; potentially curative for chemo-refractory BL patients

accessibility.rationale: Available at certified CAR-T centers; manufacturing time 2-4 weeks is challenging for fastest-growing tumor; bridging therapy needed during manufacturing; high cost

safety_profile.rationale: CRS and ICANS significant risks; Grade 3+ CRS 10-15%; Grade 3+ ICANS up to 30%; prolonged cytopenias; requires ICU-level monitoring

biomarker_match.rationale: CD19-targeted; Burkitt lymphoma universally expresses CD19; addresses chemotherapy-refractory disease through alternative killing mechanism

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Autologous T cells genetically modified to express chimeric antigen receptor targeting CD19 on malignant B cells. CAR-T cells expand in vivo and provide persistent anti-tumor immunity through direct cytotoxicity. Particularly relevant for relapsed/refractory Burkitt lymphoma unresponsive to intensive chemotherapy.

key_evidence.journal: New England Journal of Medicine / Blood Cancer Journal

treatments.name: Glofitamab (CD20xCD3 Bispecific Antibody)

evidence_level.rationale: FDA approved for R/R DLBCL; STARGLO Phase 3 shows OS benefit with GemOx; limited specific Burkitt lymphoma data but CD20-targeting applicable

survival_benefit.rationale: In R/R LBCL: CR 39% with monotherapy; median OS 25.5 months with GemOx combination in STARGLO; efficacy in high-grade B-cell lymphomas including Burkitt under investigation

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved as monotherapy; fixed-duration treatment (12 cycles); available at major cancer centers; step-up dosing protocol required

safety_profile.rationale: CRS risk especially in high-tumor-burden Burkitt (step-up dosing critical); obinutuzumab pretreatment mitigates CRS; fixed duration limits long-term toxicity

biomarker_match.rationale: CD20 universally expressed in Burkitt lymphoma; 2:1 bivalent CD20 binding provides strong target engagement; T-cell redirection effective regardless of MYC status

treatments.mechanism_of_action: CD20xCD3 T-cell-engaging bispecific antibody with novel 2:1 configuration providing bivalent CD20 binding for enhanced tumor cell engagement. Redirects patient's T cells to CD20+ tumor cells for immune-mediated killing independent of chemotherapy resistance mechanisms.

key_evidence.journal: New England Journal of Medicine / Lancet

treatments.name: Epcoritamab (Subcutaneous CD20xCD3 Bispecific Antibody)

evidence_level.rationale: FDA approved for R/R DLBCL; EPCORE NHL-1 shows durable responses; Phase 3 EPCORE DLBCL-1 confirms PFS benefit; applicable to CD20+ aggressive B-cell lymphomas

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 59%, CR 41% in R/R LBCL; 53% durable remission at 3 years; PFS HR 0.74 in Phase 3; potential salvage option for R/R Burkitt lymphoma

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved; subcutaneous administration more convenient than IV bispecifics; step-up dosing schedule; available at oncology centers

safety_profile.rationale: Subcutaneous route reduces CRS severity vs IV bispecifics; mostly Grade 1-2 CRS; lower ICANS risk; manageable safety profile

biomarker_match.rationale: CD20 universally expressed in Burkitt lymphoma; subcutaneous delivery provides sustained drug levels; effective across aggressive B-cell lymphoma subtypes

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Subcutaneously administered CD20xCD3 bispecific antibody that bridges patient's T cells to CD20-expressing tumor cells, inducing immune-mediated cytotoxicity. Subcutaneous route provides sustained plasma levels with reduced peak-related CRS risk.

treatments.name: Venetoclax + DA-EPOCH-R (BCL2 Inhibitor Combination)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 trial investigated DA-EPOCH-R +/- venetoclax in double-hit lymphomas; excess toxicity led to safety concerns; preclinical rationale strong but clinical results mixed

survival_benefit.rationale: Control arm ORR 73%, CR 67%, 15-month PFS ~65%; venetoclax arm showed excess toxicity without clear benefit; MCL1 may be more relevant target than BCL2 in Burkitt lymphoma

accessibility.rationale: Venetoclax FDA approved for CLL; off-label use in lymphoma; clinical trial access required for combination; oral administration convenient

safety_profile.rationale: Addition of venetoclax to DA-EPOCH-R resulted in excess toxicity and treatment-related deaths; TLS risk compounded (venetoclax + high tumor burden); hematologic toxicity amplified

biomarker_match.rationale: BCL2 may be less relevant in pure Burkitt lymphoma where MCL1 is the critical survival protein; more relevant for double-hit lymphomas with BCL2 rearrangement

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Venetoclax selectively inhibits BCL-2, the anti-apoptotic protein that prevents programmed cell death, combined with DA-EPOCH-R chemotherapy. Dual targeting of chemotherapy-induced DNA damage and BCL2-mediated survival signaling aims to overcome apoptotic resistance.

treatments.name: Modified R-CODOX-M/IVAC (Reduced-Intensity Magrath)

evidence_level.rationale: Multiple center studies of modified/reduced-intensity CODOX-M/IVAC; Chinese multicenter study (123 patients) with excellent outcomes; maintains efficacy with reduced toxicity

survival_benefit.rationale: Modified R-CODOX-M/IVAC: 3-year EFS 81.2%, OS 92.1% in sporadic BL; comparable efficacy to standard intensity with improved tolerability

accessibility.rationale: All components widely available; modifications reduce hospitalization and supportive care needs; suitable for centers with less intensive supportive capabilities

safety_profile.rationale: Lower toxicity than standard R-CODOX-M/IVAC; reduced methotrexate doses decrease renal toxicity; still requires intensive monitoring for TLS and myelosuppression

biomarker_match.rationale: CD20-targeted rituximab plus intensive multi-agent chemotherapy; CNS prophylaxis maintained; effective in MYC-rearranged sporadic Burkitt lymphoma

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Modified versions of the Magrath protocol with reduced doses of methotrexate and/or cytarabine while maintaining the intensive multi-agent alternating cycle approach. Modifications aim to preserve efficacy while reducing nephrotoxicity, mucositis, and prolonged myelosuppression.

treatments.name: Loncastuximab Tesirine (CD19-Directed ADC)

evidence_level.rationale: FDA approved for R/R LBCL after 2+ lines; LOTIS-2 data includes aggressive B-cell lymphomas; limited specific Burkitt lymphoma data

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 48.3%, CR 24.1% in R/R LBCL; median DOR 10.3 months; potential salvage option for R/R BL; efficacy in high-proliferation tumors under investigation

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved; IV every 3 weeks; widely available at oncology centers; no manufacturing delay unlike CAR-T

safety_profile.rationale: Hepatotoxicity (GGT/transaminase elevation); neutropenia; thrombocytopenia; edema; PBD dimer payload has potent cytotoxicity

biomarker_match.rationale: CD19-targeted; universally expressed in Burkitt lymphoma; ADC mechanism bypasses some chemotherapy resistance pathways

treatments.mechanism_of_action: CD19-directed antibody-drug conjugate carrying a pyrrolobenzodiazepine (PBD) dimer payload that crosslinks DNA upon internalization into CD19+ cells, causing potent cytotoxicity even in highly proliferative cells like Burkitt lymphoma.

treatments.name: MCL1 Inhibitor Combination Therapy (Preclinical/Early Phase)

evidence_level.rationale: Preclinical and early Phase 1 data; MCL1 identified as critical survival protein for Burkitt lymphoma; no approved MCL1 inhibitors yet; strong scientific rationale

survival_benefit.rationale: MCL1/BCL2 co-targeting is synthetically lethal in preclinical models; Burkitt lymphoma cells depend on MCL1 more than BCL2; clinical benefit not yet proven

accessibility.rationale: Investigational only; early clinical trials at select academic centers; no MCL1 inhibitors FDA-approved for any indication

safety_profile.rationale: MCL1 inhibition may cause cardiac toxicity (MCL1 critical for cardiomyocyte survival); narrow therapeutic window; dose-limiting toxicities observed in early trials

biomarker_match.rationale: MCL1 is the dominant anti-apoptotic protein in Burkitt lymphoma (more so than BCL2); specifically addresses BL biology where MYC drives MCL1 dependence

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Selective inhibitors of MCL1, the critical anti-apoptotic protein for Burkitt lymphoma cell survival. Unlike BCL2, MCL1 represents the primary survival dependency in MYC-driven tumors. Combination with BCL2 inhibitors (venetoclax) may be synthetically lethal.

clinical_trials.title: R-CODOX-M/R-IVAC versus DA-EPOCH-R in Newly Diagnosed Burkitt Lymphoma
clinical_trials.early_results: DA-EPOCH-R non-inferior to R-CODOX-M/IVAC with fewer toxicities; 2-year PFS 70% vs 76%; DA-EPOCH-R validated as standard option

clinical_trials.title: Multicenter Study of Risk-Adapted DA-EPOCH-R in Adults with Untreated Burkitt Lymphoma
clinical_trials.early_results: 4-year EFS 84.5%, OS 87.0%; low-risk 100% EFS; cures most adults with BL regardless of HIV status

clinical_trials.title: Modified R-CODOX-M/IVAC in Burkitt Lymphoma
clinical_trials.early_results: 2-year PFS 77.2%, OS 80.7% with rituximab addition to CODOX-M/IVAC

clinical_trials.title: CAR-T Cell Therapy for Relapsed/Refractory Burkitt Lymphoma
clinical_trials.early_results: Case series show responses in chemotherapy-resistant BL; sequential dual-antigen CAR-T under investigation

clinical_trials.title: Glofitamab/Epcoritamab in Aggressive B-Cell Lymphomas Including Burkitt
clinical_trials.early_results: Bispecific antibodies show activity in high-grade B-cell lymphomas; Burkitt-specific data accumulating

combination_strategies.rationale: Mandatory CNS prophylaxis reduces CNS relapse from 30-50% to 6-11%; CSF involvement is the strongest negative prognostic factor; intrathecal therapy integrated into each chemotherapy cycle

Burkitt lymphoma is the highest-risk malignancy for TLS due to 24-hour doubling time and Ki-67 ~100%; rasburicase reduces uric acid within 4 hours; hydration at 3L/m2/day prevents renal precipitation; must begin BEFORE chemotherapy initiation

Addition of rituximab to Magrath protocol improved 5-year OS from 50% to 74% (p=0.03); rituximab addresses CD20+ tumor cells through ADCC/CDC; now standard to include rituximab with all intensive BL regimens

Co-targeting MCL1 and BCL2 is synthetically lethal in preclinical BL models; MCL1 is the dominant anti-apoptotic protein in Burkitt lymphoma; dual blockade may overcome apoptotic resistance in refractory disease
STARGLO demonstrated OS benefit with glofitamab+GemOx; bispecific T-cell engagement combined with chemotherapy provides dual killing mechanisms; may overcome chemoresistance in relapsed BL

supportive_care.evidence: NCCN/ASCO Category 1 recommendation for high-risk TLS (Burkitt lymphoma is highest risk); rasburicase + aggressive hydration + electrolyte monitoring mandatory
supportive_care.benefit: Prevents life-threatening TLS-related renal failure, cardiac arrhythmias, and seizures; reduces TLS mortality from 15-20% to <5%; rasburicase reduces uric acid within 4 hours; must begin 12-24 hours before chemotherapy

supportive_care.evidence: NCCN mandates G-CSF for all intensive BL regimens; antimicrobial prophylaxis (fluoroquinolone, antifungal, anti-PJP) during neutropenic periods
supportive_care.benefit: Reduces febrile neutropenia duration and infection-related mortality; enables dose-dense chemotherapy scheduling critical for preventing tumor regrowth between cycles

supportive_care.evidence: ASCO Fertility Preservation Guidelines; intensive BL regimens contain high cumulative alkylating agent doses causing near-certain gonadal damage in males
supportive_care.benefit: All intensive BL regimens are highly gonadotoxic; sperm banking before treatment is simple, rapid, and preserves reproductive options; must be performed before first cycle even if delaying treatment by 24-48 hours

supportive_care.evidence: Both DA-EPOCH-R and R-CODOX-M/IVAC contain doxorubicin; cumulative anthracycline dose monitoring required; baseline and post-treatment LVEF assessment
supportive_care.benefit: Early detection of anthracycline-induced cardiomyopathy; dose modification if LVEF drops below threshold; 45-year-old male at moderate cardiac risk with intensive anthracycline regimens

supportive_care.evidence: Intensive BL regimens cause severe mucositis (Grade 3-4 in 20-40% of patients); nutritional compromise impairs treatment tolerance and immune recovery
supportive_care.benefit: Proactive mucositis prevention (cryotherapy, palifermin consideration) and nutritional support maintain treatment intensity; parenteral nutrition may be required during severe mucositis episodes

supportive_care.approach: Psychosocial Support and Treatment Urgency Counseling
supportive_care.evidence: Burkitt lymphoma requires emergency treatment initiation; rapid disease progression creates acute psychological distress; NCCN Distress Management Guidelines
supportive_care.benefit: Addresses extreme anxiety from diagnosis of fastest-growing human tumor; facilitates informed consent under time pressure; supports treatment adherence during intensive multi-cycle regimen

sources.title: Low-Intensity Therapy in Adults with Burkitt's Lymphoma (DA-EPOCH-R NCI Study) - NEJM

Evaluation of Rituximab Addition to CODOX-M/IVAC for Burkitt's Lymphoma - Annals of Oncology
Modified R-CODOX-M/IVAC in Chinese Patients with Sporadic Burkitt Lymphoma - Cancer Biology & Medicine
Long-term Outcome of Hyper-CVAD-R for Burkitt Leukemia/Lymphoma - Blood Advances

DA-EPOCH-R for Adult Burkitt's Lymphoma: Pros and Cons - JCO Oncology Practice
Outcomes and Prognostic Factors in Burkitt Lymphoma/Leukemia - Blood Cancer Journal
